text.string: In the middle of the scene, <|object_ref_start|>a red backpack on the chair<|object_ref_end|><|box_start|>(010,020),(110,120)<|box_end|> looks slightly worn, and to the right there are <|object_ref_start|>two shiny apples on the table<|object_ref_end|><|box_start|>(200,210),(260,280)<|box_end|><|box_start|>(300,310),(360,380)<|box_end|> that catch the light; the caption continues with some extra description about the room and the lighting, and then a detection snippet appears without any mention <|box_start|>(400,410),(450,460)<|box_end|> which should be treated as stray; finally it mentions <|object_ref_start|>the small label near the corner<|object_ref_end|> but gives no box for it at all.
Round 2: same box but different ref

Now I refer to the exact same region as before but with a different phrase: <|object_ref_start|>the worn bag on the seat<|object_ref_end|><|box_start|>(010,020),(110,120)<|box_end|> and I will stop here.
